description: Figure 20 presents the experimental and simulated microstructure evolutions of low-alloy steel during hot deformation at 950 °C with a transient strain-rate decrease from 0.1 s⁻¹ to 0.01 s⁻¹ at a true strain of 0.4. Panel (a) shows EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) maps (top row) and grain orientation spread (GOS) maps (bottom row) at three stages: after the first stage at 0.4 strain (left, average grain size 81.50±6.32 μm, DRX fraction 38.9%), at 0.5 strain following the rate drop (middle, 52.92±3.35 μm, DRX fraction 95.2%), and at 0.9 strain (right, 50.71±3.29 μm, DRX fraction 97.7%). Panel (b) displays the corresponding 3D cellular automaton simulation results, where white grains represent the matrix, green grains denote the first stage (950 °C, 0.1 s⁻¹), and red grains denote the second stage (950 °C, 0.01 s⁻¹). The simulated DRX fractions increase from 42.9% at 0.4 strain to 99.1% at 0.5 strain and 99.8% at 0.9 strain, with average grain sizes of 82.68 μm, 50.78 μm, and 52.58 μm, respectively. The figure demonstrates that decreasing the strain rate triggers rapid dynamic recrystallization and grain refinement, and that the 3D CA model successfully captures this multi-stage recrystallization behavior.
